baseline.final_answer: Printers can print white by not applying any ink to the paper. While cartridges contain black, yellow, cyan, and magenta ink, the printer can choose to leave areas of the paper blank to represent white.

evaluation.feedback: The answer claims printers print white by not applying ink, but this is not supported by any of the provided documents. The documents discuss various ink colors and cartridge types but do not explain how white is achieved.
